source: D67097 HAL 4.0 Summative Usability Evaluation Plan Rev01.docx.pdf
title: 9.0 List of Tasks > 9.1 Use-related Risk Analysis
text_content: D56979 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Report identifies safety-related use errors, their design mitigators, and the risk control operator for the design mitigators. Design mitigators where a human is the risk control operator are considered for inclusion in the summative usability testing.  Design mitigators that identify the risk control operators as the device are not included in the summative testing. Hazard-related use scenarios to be included in the summative test were identified from these reports.  The product team selected a subset of the human factor related hazard-related use scenarios based on the severity of the potential harm that could be caused by use error (e.g., for which medical intervention would be needed). During the risk hazard analysis meetings, the team identified which risks have a human factor (HF) cause type. All HF risks and their related risk control measures will be tested by the design validation team, whereas the UX team will focus on

text_content: The Manufacturing NPI Project Manager is accountable for downloading the Manufacturing Plan template, completing product development team level information (Product config, Scope, etc.) and maintaining the Manufacturing Plan. The Manufacturing NPI Project Manager is ultimately the lead on all manufacturing deliverables/activities. The Manufacturing NPI Project Manager will collect and collate content for the Manufacturing Operations Plan from across the entire project environment and the applicable organizational environments. As decisions are made regarding how the product should be implemented in manufacturing, a version of the Manufacturing Plan should be updated to reflect those changes. A new version should be released as determined in previous recommendation. By Design Freeze Review the Manufacturing Plan should, at a minimum, address every deliverable in the Design Transfer Checklist for which Manufacturing Operations is responsible.

The Manufacturing NPI Project Manager will collect and collate content for the Manufacturing Operations Plan from across the entire project environment and the applicable organizational environments. As decisions are made regarding how the product should be implemented in manufacturing, a version of the Manufacturing Plan should be updated to reflect those changes. A new version should be released as determined in previous recommendation. By Design Freeze Review the Manufacturing Plan should, at a minimum, address every deliverable in the Design Transfer Checklist for which Manufacturing Operations is responsible.

text_content: D56979 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Report identifies safety-related use errors, their design mitigators, and the risk control operator for the design mitigators. Design mitigators where a human is the risk control operator are considered for inclusion in the summative usability testing.  Design mitigators that identify the risk control operators as the device are not included in the summative testing. Hazard-related use scenarios to be included in the summative test were identified from these reports.  The product team selected a subset of the human factor related hazard-related use scenarios based on the severity of the potential harm that could be caused by use error (e.g., for which medical intervention would be needed). During the risk hazard analysis meetings, the team identified which risks have a human factor (HF) cause type. title: 3.2.1 Scope, Schedule and Budget Control (Continued)
text_content: Impacts the program schedule committed to MER by more than two calendar weeks Schedule control will be maintained by recording project tasks in TFS with one backlog per team defined in section 2.1 Project Roles, Team and Responsibilities.  Each team backlog will be ordered in such a way that each task maps to an EPIC in which that deliverable is applicable. EPICs are program milestones and are defined in section 3.7 Main Milestones. Project schedule will be tracked by analysis of the tasks remaining in each team’s backlog in TFS and the EPIC to which it reports.  Burndown charts will be made for each team and each EPIC allowing a projection to be made on EPIC completion by team based on that team’s average velocity – see Figure 3.  Program level burndowns will also be made for each EPIC which combine the progress of the program towards an EPIC – see Figure 4.  Changes in scope, schedule and budget will be escalated to the Business Scrum as well as external stakeholders, as needed.

This UAP describes GPL-relevant organization and UA-specific organization where it is not included in the DDP/SDP (or when including it in the UAP facilitates project communication). The Library CD/DVD  included for some projects is not listed in this table, as it compiles the UA. Reviewers are subject-matter experts (SME) who are integral to product development. They contribute to content development via internal reviews of drafts, and through demos and discussions. Reviewers consult and contribute (C). Formal Reviewers participate in the Formal Review phase of documents. They do not sign approval. Formal reviewers consult and contribute (C). Stakeholders have interest in the project or deliverable but are not reviewers or approvers. Stakeholders consult and contribute (C).

source: D67097 HAL 4.0 Summative Usability Evaluation Plan Rev01.docx.pdf
title: 9.0 List of Tasks > 9.1 Use-related Risk Analysis
text_content: D56979 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Report identifies safety-related use errors, their design mitigators, and the risk control operator for the design mitigators. Design mitigators where a human is the risk control operator are considered for inclusion in the summative usability testing.  Design mitigators that identify the risk control operators as the device are not included in the summative testing. Hazard-related use scenarios to be included in the summative test were identified from these reports.  The product team selected a subset of the human factor related hazard-related use scenarios based on the severity of the potential harm that could be caused by use error (e.g., for which medical intervention would be needed). During the risk hazard analysis meetings, the team identified which risks have a human factor (HF) cause type. All HF risks and their related risk control measures will be tested by the design validation team, whereas the UX team will focus on
